name: Testbed Smoke Capture Validation And Push

messages.content:
I’ll verify from the current workspace state rather than assume the pushed commits are enough. I’ll sync refs first, then run the relevant build/test path and a visual testbed smoke check if the environment can capture it.

The only dirty paths are `.cozter` session/upload artifacts; the project code tree is clean. I’m fetching `origin` now, then I’ll run the native test targets against the current `main`.

`origin` fetched cleanly and the branch is still aligned. I’m going to build the testbed and the graphics-related test targets together, then run the matching CTest selection and the smoke capture.

The standards-facing quick check (`git diff --check`) is clean. The build is running now; it had to regenerate some Ninja state, then started compiling normally.

The native build is past the core graphics/Vulkan objects and still progressing. I’m waiting for the final link/tests rather than interrupting it.

The build completed cleanly, including asset cooking and linking `testbed`, `ecs_graphics_tests`, and `assets_graphics_tests`. Next I’m running the two focused test binaries through CTest and checking the display environment for the visual smoke run.

The focused CTest run passed. The shell still has no display variables exported, but there is an X socket at `:0` and a Wayland socket at `/run/user/1000/wayland-0`, so I’m going to run the smoke capture with explicit display settings.

I found the active Xwayland server and its Xauthority file. I’ll use that explicit `DISPLAY=:0`/`XAUTHORITY` pair so the testbed smoke script can see the native window instead of skipping because the shell environment is empty.

The visual smoke run started but the testbed exited before capture with signal 5. Since build and unit tests are clean, I’m checking the fresh log output and launch assumptions before deciding whether this is an environment/display issue or an app regression.

The crash is from a bad launch path, not the renderer: the log shows the app mounted `__exec/linux/x64/testbed/dbg/res`, while the build cooked assets to `__exec/linux/x64/testbed/res`; then it failed loading `project/characters/imported_deformable`. I’m rerunning with the repo root working directory so the asset volume resolves the same way the build produced it.

The loader confirms the ordering: it prefers `$cwd/res` first, then the executable’s `dbg/res`, then parent `res`. Because there is an old `dbg/res` volume, the smoke test needs `cwd=__exec/linux/x64/testbed` to force the freshly cooked parent `res` volume.

With the correct asset mount, the app got farther but now exits with signal 11 before capture. I’m pulling the fresh log and will try a simpler launch without the CSG interaction to separate startup/render health from the edit smoke path.

The corrected run mounted the right volume and completed startup, then crashed after the first visible frames while using Mesa llvmpipe. I’m going to retry with hardware Vulkan (`--software-vulkan off`) and first do a plain capture before adding CSG input back in.

The plain native window capture passes on hardware Vulkan. I’m now rerunning the same smoke path with the surface-edit preview input so the recent CSG/render changes are exercised visually too.

The CSG preview smoke capture also passed with hardware Vulkan. I’m converting the BMP to PNG for easier viewing and then I’ll do one final status check.

The script’s BMP is valid, but the direct X11 capture still shows the known Vulkan/Xwayland partial-black behavior in the lower region. I’m going to grab a compositor-level screenshot as proof as well, since that is the more reliable visual check for this setup.

The hardware run log shows the full startup path and CSG preview becoming active on the AMD device. I’m launching the testbed once more briefly and using Spectacle fullscreen capture, which reads from the compositor rather than direct X11 window pixels.

The compositor screenshot looks correct: the native `NWBLoader` window is visible with the testbed scene rendered. I’m doing one more pass on the optional llvmpipe path because the smoke script’s `auto` mode selects it here and that path crashed even though the AMD hardware path passes.

The checked-in tree is still aligned with `origin/main`; only `.cozter` session/upload files are dirty. One caveat from verification: the real AMD Vulkan path passes, but the optional lavapipe software ICD path still exits during first render, so I’m taking a quick noninteractive backtrace to make that failure concrete.

Checked. The real AMD Vulkan path is working.

What passed:
- `git fetch origin`; `main` is even with `origin/main`
- `git diff --check` clean
- Build: `testbed`, `nwb_ecs_graphics_tests`, `nwb_assets_graphics_tests`
- CTest: `nwb_ecs_graphics_tests` and `nwb_assets_graphics_tests` both passed
- Native testbed smoke capture on hardware Vulkan passed, including CSG preview
- Compositor screenshot confirms the window renders correctly

Caveat: the optional Mesa lavapipe software Vulkan path still crashes during first render. GDB shows the crash inside `libvulkan_lvp.so` / `libLLVM.so.22.1` during sampler/shader work, not in project code. Hardware Vulkan is fine.

Only dirty files left are `.cozter` session/upload artifacts. No `testbed`, `logserver`, or `gdb` processes are still running.

[[attach: __artifacts/testbed_working_check_spectacle_fullscreen.png]]
so, is there a thing that we have to fix that means?

No code fix is indicated.

What we know:
- `main` is pushed and aligned with `origin/main`.
- Standards check passed.
- Relevant native builds/tests passed.
- The remaining dirty state is only `.cozter` session/upload artifacts, not project code.

The only cleanup worth considering is housekeeping: either ignore or remove `.cozter` artifacts if you want `git status` fully clean. It does not mean the app/build needs a fix.

I’ll use the GitHub publish workflow and re-check the worktree first. If the only pending files are `.cozter` session artifacts, I won’t commit them unless they’re the thing you explicitly want preserved in Git.

There is now one project file modified again: `tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py`. I’m fetching remote state and inspecting that diff; `.cozter` files are still session/upload artifacts, so I’ll keep them out of the commit.

The only code change is small: the smoke script now defaults `--software-vulkan` to `off` instead of `auto`. I’m running the relevant script/format checks before I stage it.

`py_compile` passed and `git diff --check` is clean. The repo standard is mostly C++-source focused; this Python smoke file keeps its existing LF style, and this edit does not introduce whitespace or EOF churn.

I started a `testbed` build because this option controls the smoke launcher behavior. Once that target is current, I’ll run the smoke CTest entry or the equivalent script path, then commit only the smoke-file change.

The build is still compiling normally after regenerating Ninja state. Nothing has been staged yet; I’m keeping the commit boundary to the one smoke script change.

`testbed` built successfully, including a fresh asset cook. I’m running the registered smoke test next; if the display backend is unavailable it should skip with code 77, otherwise it will exercise the capture path.

CTest skipped because no `DISPLAY`/`WAYLAND_DISPLAY` is exported in this shell. I’m checking the session sockets now so I can run the smoke path against the real compositor if one is available, instead of accepting the skip as proof.

There is a live Wayland socket and Xwayland socket. I’m rerunning the smoke test with `WAYLAND_DISPLAY=wayland-0` and `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/1000` so the script can actually launch and capture.

CTest still reports a skip without showing the skip reason. I’m invoking the smoke script directly with the built executable and the same working directory/output shape so we can see the backend decision clearly.

The smoke script’s active capture backend here is X11; Wayland alone makes it skip with `XOpenDisplay failed`. I found the Xwayland socket and xauth file, so I’m rerunning with `DISPLAY=:0` and that Xauthority.

The direct smoke run passed with the new default and captured a real 1280x900 window image at `__artifacts/testbed_smoke_default_system_vulkan.bmp`. I’m staging only `tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py` now, leaving `.cozter` and the capture artifact uncommitted.

The staged diff is exactly the two-line default/help update for `--software-vulkan`. I’m committing that now on `main`, then I’ll push to `origin/main`.

Push completed: `main` advanced from `fabd327` to `b58c270` on `origin/main`. I’m doing a final status check so the remaining dirty state is explicit.

Committed and pushed `b58c270 Default smoke capture to system Vulkan` to `origin/main`.

Validated with:
- `git diff --check`
- `python3 -m py_compile tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py`
- `cmake --build --preset linux-clang-testbed-dbg --target testbed`
- Direct smoke capture passed with `DISPLAY=:0` and produced a real 1280x900 screenshot.

Remaining uncommitted files are only `.cozter` session/upload artifacts, left out intentionally.

[[attach: __artifacts/testbed_smoke_default_system_vulkan.png]]
and proceed the next step please

I’ll continue from the commit/push path and confirm the branch state now. First I’m checking whether there is anything left to commit and whether `main` is still aligned with `origin/main`.

The pushed commit is already at `HEAD` and `origin/main`: `b58c270 Default smoke capture to system Vulkan`. I’m doing one remote fetch check now so the final state is based on the server, not just local refs.

Done. I fetched `origin` and confirmed `main` is pushed and aligned:

`b58c270 Default smoke capture to system Vulkan`

There is no project code left to commit. The only remaining dirty paths are local `.cozter` session/upload artifacts, and I left them out of Git.

summary:
After confirming `main` was aligned with `origin/main`, you asked to ensure repo standards compliance and that everything “works fine,” then to commit/push any remaining needed fixes.

Work focused on validating the pushed code via native builds + focused CTest runs, then running the GUI smoke capture by explicitly setting display env vars (the shell had none exported). The first smoke attempts failed due to an incorrect launch/working-directory asset mount (assets were being resolved from an old `dbg/res` volume instead of the freshly cooked `res`), then a crash occurred on the Mesa software Vulkan path; hardware Vulkan runs and captures succeeded, and a compositor-level screenshot was taken to avoid known X11/Vulkan capture artifacts.

Finally, you requested another commit/push: a small change in `tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py` to default `--software-vulkan` to `off` (system Vulkan). Validated with `python3 -m py_compile`, `git diff --check`, `cmake --build ... --target testbed`, and a direct smoke run with explicit `DISPLAY`/`XAUTHORITY`, producing a real capture; committed and pushed as `b58c270`. Remaining dirty state is only local `.cozter` session/upload artifacts and uncommitted captures.
long_term: User expects a real visual screenshot as proof the native `testbed` is working correctly.
The project is a native CMake build with a `testbed` target and smoke capture coverage in `tests/smoke/testbed_window_capture_smoke.py`.
In this environment, `DISPLAY`/`WAYLAND_DISPLAY` may not be exported even when Xwayland/Wayland sessions exist, so running/capturing may require explicitly setting them (and `XAUTHORITY` for X11).
Direct X11 `XGetImage(window)` can return blank or partially black for Vulkan-presented content under compositors/Xwayland; compositor-level capture is more reliable for verification screenshots.
The X11 smoke capture script was hardened to fail blank/white images and to retry using a root-screen-region capture for the raised window when direct window reads are blank.
The smoke capture script now defaults `--software-vulkan` to `off` (system Vulkan) due to instability/crashes on the Mesa software Vulkan path in this setup.
User preference: pull/sync from git first before debugging or changing behavior.
When launching `testbed`, the working directory matters (wrong `cwd` can trigger missing-asset failures even if the backend is correct).
Repo coding standards are documented in `.helper/standard.md`, including enforced CRLF line endings and strict EOF rules.
`.cozter` session/upload artifacts should be kept out of commits unless explicitly required.
The GitHub CLI (`gh`) is not installed in this environment, so PR creation must use other means.
